message:
Harden daemon protocol tests, SFTP username validation, and asciicast stdin lifecycle

Three independent hardening changes from proactive audit:

1. daemon/protocol.rs — Eliminate panic-based test assertions
   Replace `panic!("expected ...")` branches in 6 message round-trip
   tests with assert!(matches!()) + if-let guards so failures produce
   proper assertion diagnostics instead of panics (addresses UBS criticals).

2. sources/sync.rs — Validate non-empty SSH username before SFTP connect
   Introduce normalize_username() that rejects blank/whitespace-only
   candidates. Walk the resolution chain (user@host → SSH config → USER
   → LOGNAME) through the normalizer. If all sources are empty, fail
   fast with a descriptive PathSyncResult error instead of silently
   connecting as "nobody".

3. tui_asciicast.rs — Deterministic stdin forwarder shutdown
   The detached stdin-forwarding thread could outlive the TUI child
   process and briefly consume subsequent terminal input. Add:
   - AtomicBool stop flag checked each read iteration
   - StdinNonBlockingGuard (Unix) that temporarily sets O_NONBLOCK on
     stdin fd so the blocked read() returns WouldBlock, allowing the
     thread to observe the stop flag and exit promptly
   - RAII guard restores original fd flags on drop
   - WouldBlock handler sleeps 10ms to avoid busy-spin
   - On non-Unix platforms, behavior falls back to detach-on-drop

Beads issue tracker synced with new closed issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
